title: [ipv6_addr.h:64-87] struct in6_addr detection cascade order can cause false positives
description:
Category not checked: Preprocessor detection reliability

Bug found: The in6_addr detection at ipv6_addr.h lines 64-87 has a potential issue:

Line 72 checks for __APPLE__ && _STRUCT_IN6_ADDR. The _STRUCT_IN6_ADDR macro is defined BY Apple's headers when struct in6_addr is defined. But if someone includes ipv6_addr.h BEFORE including Apple's netinet/in.h:

1. __APPLE__ is defined (it's a compiler predefined)
2. _STRUCT_IN6_ADDR is NOT yet defined (netinet/in.h not included)
3. Check at line 72 fails (__APPLE__ true but _STRUCT_IN6_ADDR false)
4. Falls through to line 74 (none of the above)
5. Defines fallback struct in6_addr
6. Later, user includes netinet/in.h
7. Apple's netinet/in.h defines struct in6_addr again
8. COMPILE ERROR: redefinition

The fix would be to check __APPLE__ alone (without _STRUCT_IN6_ADDR) and always include the system header on Apple platforms. Or require users to include netinet/in.h first.

Impact: P3 - Compile error on macOS if headers included in wrong order.
close_reason: Fixed: Removed _SYS_SOCKET_H from detection cascade - sys/socket.h does not define struct in6_addr (that's in netinet/in.h), so checking it caused false positives. Added documentation explaining why it was removed.
